skill.name: visioncraft
skill.description: Generate images and videos through Agnes AI using PowerShell scripts for text-to-image, image-to-image, text-to-video, image-to-video, multi-image video, and keyframe animation.
skill.summary: Create images and short videos from prompts or reference images using Agnes AI.
skill.author: BAI-32

security_audit.summary: Static analysis reported many command, network, environment, filesystem, and weak-crypto patterns. Most documentation hits are false positives from Markdown examples, backticks, URLs, and words such as Base64, but the executable PowerShell scripts do make authenticated requests to Agnes AI. No prompt injection attempt or confirmed malicious intent was found, so this should publish only with clear disclosure about third-party API use and API key handling.

medium_findings.title: Authenticated Third-Party API Calls
medium_findings.description: The image and video scripts send prompts, reference image URLs, and bearer-token authentication to Agnes AI endpoints. This is intentional functionality, but users must know that generation data leaves the local environment.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The scripts clearly define Agnes AI URLs and use PowerShell web request cmdlets with authorization headers. The behavior is documented as core functionality, so the risk is disclosure and data handling rather than hidden exfiltration.

medium_findings.title: Environment API Key Access
medium_findings.description: The scripts read AGNES_API_KEY from the environment and place it in Authorization headers. This is normal for an API client, but the skill depends on a secret available to the shell session.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: Direct environment-variable reads and bearer header construction are visible in the executable scripts. The documentation also instructs users to keep the key in the environment, which supports legitimate intent.

medium_findings.title: PowerShell Script Execution Surface
medium_findings.description: The skill instructs the assistant to run local PowerShell scripts for media generation. Parameters are typed and no Invoke-Expression pattern was found, but marketplace users should understand that this skill executes scripts.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The skill explicitly calls scripts and the files define PowerShell parameters. I did not find evidence of dynamic command construction, which lowers the severity from high to medium.

low_findings.title: Documentation Pattern False Positives
low_findings.description: Many static hits come from Markdown command examples, URLs, and placeholder API key instructions. These are not executable by themselves and do not show malicious behavior.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The cited content is documentation or sample commands, not hidden runtime logic. It still matters because users may copy the examples, but it does not confirm malicious intent.

low_findings.title: Filesystem and Weak-Crypto Hits Dismissed
low_findings.description: The reported path traversal and weak cryptography patterns appear to be from documentation paths, Base64 response descriptions, and parameter rules. No evidence found of filesystem traversal or cryptographic decision logic in the executable scripts.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The locations are documentation describing relative links, data URI support, or response fields. I did not find code that opens arbitrary local paths or implements weak cryptography.

dangerous_patterns.title: Network Requests with Bearer Token
dangerous_patterns.description: PowerShell web request cmdlets send a bearer token to Agnes AI. This is expected for the API wrapper, but it is a sensitive pattern that requires user consent and clear vendor trust.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The code directly combines network calls with authorization headers. The endpoint is consistent with the documented Agnes AI service.

dangerous_patterns.title: Assistant-Invoked Local Scripts
dangerous_patterns.description: The skill workflow asks the assistant to run local PowerShell scripts. This is a known execution surface even though the reviewed scripts use structured parameters and do not show command injection.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The skill instructions and script parameter blocks confirm the execution surface. Severity is limited by the absence of dynamic shell evaluation in the reviewed code.

content.user_title: Generate Images and Videos with Agnes AI
content.value_statement: Creative teams need fast visual drafts without writing API calls by hand. VisionCraft turns natural language prompts into Agnes AI image and video requests with reusable scripts.
actual_capabilities: Generates images from text prompts through the Agnes image API.
Transforms an existing image URL with image-to-image prompts.
Creates short videos from text prompts through the Agnes video API.
Animates one or more reference images into video clips.
Supports keyframe transitions between multiple image references.
Prints a MEDIA URL when generation succeeds.
limitations: Requires PowerShell 7 or compatible PowerShell execution.
Requires a valid AGNES_API_KEY environment variable.
Sends prompts and reference URLs to Agnes AI endpoints.
Video generation can take several minutes and may time out locally.

use_cases.title: Campaign Visual Drafts
use_cases.description: Create poster, banner, and social media image concepts from product or campaign prompts.

use_cases.title: Prototype Motion Concepts
use_cases.description: Generate short cinematic clips that test movement, framing, and atmosphere before production.

use_cases.title: Reference Image Animation
use_cases.description: Animate portraits, product shots, or scene references while preserving the main composition.

prompt_templates.title: Create a Basic Image
prompt_templates.prompt: Generate an image of [subject] in [style], with [lighting], [background], and [mood]. Use a [ratio] aspect ratio.

prompt_templates.title: Restyle a Reference Image
prompt_templates.prompt: Transform this image URL: [image URL]. Preserve [important elements] and change the scene to [new style or setting].

prompt_templates.title: Create a Short Video
prompt_templates.prompt: Create a [seconds]-second video of [subject] doing [action]. Use [camera motion], [lighting], and [visual style].

prompt_templates.title: Animate Keyframes
prompt_templates.prompt: Use these keyframes: [first image URL] and [second image URL]. Create a smooth transition with [motion], [pace], and [continuity requirements].

output_examples.input: Generate a square product image for a ceramic coffee mug on a clean studio background.
output_examples.output: The skill returns a hosted image link and a short success message with the selected size and generation time.

output_examples.input: Animate this portrait with subtle breathing, soft hair movement, and warm background light.
output_examples.output: The skill creates a video task, polls until completion, and returns a hosted video link when available.

output_examples.input: Create a smooth transition between two landscape images for an opening scene.
output_examples.output: The skill uses keyframe mode and returns a video link that morphs between the supplied reference images.

best_practices: Use public image URLs or valid data URIs for reference inputs.
Describe subject, style, lighting, camera, and motion in one focused prompt.
Check API key scope and vendor terms before using sensitive prompts.
anti_patterns: Do not put real API keys in prompts, scripts, examples, or commits.
Do not send private images or confidential prompts without vendor approval.
Do not expect exact frame timing or resolution when the service normalizes outputs.

faq.question: What does VisionCraft generate?
faq.answer: It generates images and short videos through Agnes AI using prompt-driven PowerShell scripts.

faq.question: Does it require an API key?
faq.answer: Yes. It reads AGNES_API_KEY from the environment and sends it as a bearer token.

faq.question: Can it animate existing images?
faq.answer: Yes. It supports one image, multiple image references, and keyframe animation mode.

faq.question: Does it store generated files locally?
faq.answer: The reviewed instructions return hosted media URLs and say not to download files unless the user asks.

faq.question: Is it safe to use with confidential content?
faq.answer: Use caution. Prompts and reference URLs are sent to Agnes AI, so sensitive content needs prior approval.

faq.question: Which tools can use this skill?
faq.answer: The report lists Claude, Codex, and Claude Code as supported tools.
